hypothesis: Incorporating SEM images from intermediate cycle counts (e.g., 25, 50, and 75 cycles) as an auxiliary pretraining domain between the additive-free source domain and the additive-containing target domain will create a progressive transfer learning curriculum that reduces the feature distribution gap more gradually, thereby enabling the model to achieve higher classification accuracy on additive-containing aged cathode classes (formation and 100-cycle states) with fewer than 50 labeled target-domain samples compared to the two-stage framework presented in this paper.
